title: Code index, inbox, and benchmark gates harden Kage handoff quality
summary: Fact: Kage now has a first class memory inbox report, an LSP compatible code index command, CI/PR/sync workflow steps that generate that index before refresh, and benchmark gates with an overall score. Why: Audit and PR
body:
Fact: Kage now has a first-class memory inbox report, an LSP-compatible code-index command, CI/PR/sync workflow steps that generate that index before refresh, and benchmark gates with an overall score. Why: Audit and PR handoff should expose actionable review work, precise source-index coverage, and benchmark pass/fail gates instead of loose proof metrics. Trigger: When changing audit, viewer, CI, benchmark, or PR handoff behavior. Action: Run `kage code-index --project .` before `kage refresh --project .`, use `kage inbox --project .` for review work, and treat benchmark gates as the proof boundary. Verified by: npm test in mcp; node mcp/dist/cli.js audit --project . --json. Risk if forgotten: Future agents may rely on stale AST-only code graphs, scattered review artifacts, or marketing metrics without explicit gates.
Evidence: Implemented writeLspSymbolIndex/kage_code_index, memoryInbox/kage_inbox, viewer inbox loading, CI code-index steps, benchmark gates, and audit precision denominator fix. Tests passed with 73/73; root audit reports validation warnings 0 and precise source coverage 100%.
Verified by: npm test in mcp; node mcp/dist/cli.js audit --project . --json
